sourceMovie.title: Extinction
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Extinction for the same reason it broke through in 2018: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ben Young keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gemini Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Extinction clicked for you, Gemini Man (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Extinction?
faq.answer: Try Gemini Man, They Live, Soldier—each shares DNA with Extinction while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Extinction?
faq.answer: Extinction (2018) is best described as Action, Drama, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Extinction worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
